explicit_text: I served my country as a member of the United States Marine Corps for twenty years before retiring. That experience shaped a lot of who I am and gave me a strong sense of discipline and service. After retiring, I came back to my community and eventually ran for public office, carrying those values with me into my role as mayor. My time in the Marines was foundational to my commitment to serve and lead in Mount Sterling.

That experience shaped a lot of who I am and gave me a strong sense of discipline and service. My time in this elite military force was foundational to my commitment to serve and lead in Mount Sterling.

explicit_text: My relationship with The Post Clinic has been deeply tied to my role as mayor, where I've worked hard to help grow the clinic and secure funding for a new building. The clinic serves a vital role for our community and surrounding counties, especially for those who are uninsured or underinsured. I've seen firsthand how it provides care to people who otherwise have nowhere to turn, including an exchange student who needed a physical to play sports. The Post Clinic is a place where patients feel like family and trust their providers, and it’s been one of the most rewarding projects I’ve been involved with as an elected official.

explicit_text: I was born and raised right here in Mount Sterling, Kentucky, and I’m proud to call it home. After serving in the Marines and spending twenty years away, I returned and have now served as mayor for seven years. Mount Sterling is a community with great people and a strong spirit, and it stands out because of projects like The Post Clinic that serve not only our city but over a dozen surrounding counties. I believe Mount Sterling is one of the finest cities in the Commonwealth, and I’m honored to represent and serve its citizens every day.
implicit_text: I was born and raised right here in the city known as the county seat of Montgomery County, Kentucky, and I’m proud to call it home. After serving in the Marines and spending twenty years away, I returned and have now served as mayor for seven years. This community is filled with great people and a strong spirit, and it stands out because of projects like the local health clinic that serve not only our city but over a dozen surrounding counties. I believe our city is one of the finest in the Commonwealth, and I’m honored to represent and serve its citizens every day.

explicit_text: One of the challenges with the old Post Clinic building was that it was not ADA compliant, which limited accessibility for some patients. As mayor, I worked to help secure funding for a new facility that meets ADA standards, ensuring everyone in our community can access the care they need. This was an important part of making the clinic a welcoming and inclusive place for all, including vulnerable populations. Compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act was a key factor in the clinic’s growth and ability to serve more people effectively.
implicit_text: One of the challenges with the old community health center was that it was not compliant with the federal law ensuring accessibility for individuals with disabilities, which limited access for some patients. As mayor, I worked to help secure funding for a new facility that meets those standards, ensuring everyone in our community can access the care they need. This was an important part of making the clinic a welcoming and inclusive place for all, including vulnerable populations. Compliance with this landmark civil rights legislation was a key factor in the clinic’s growth and ability to serve more people effectively.

explicit_text: The Amish population in our area is one of the groups that benefit from The Post Clinic because they often fall outside traditional insurance coverage. The clinic provides a vital service to them and others who might not have access to healthcare otherwise. It’s rewarding to see how the clinic fills gaps in our community’s healthcare system, especially for populations like the Amish who have unique needs. This inclusivity is part of what makes The Post Clinic so important to Mount Sterling and the surrounding counties.

explicit_text: Even though the Affordable Care Act was designed to ensure everyone has health coverage, I’ve seen that gaps still remain in our community. The Post Clinic continues to see many people every day who are uninsured or underinsured despite the ACA. This shows that while the law helps, it doesn’t solve all the problems, and facilities like The Post Clinic are still essential to fill those gaps. As mayor, I’m proud that our community offers this resource to help people who fall through the cracks of the broader healthcare system.
